D2 Vision L5 Evaluate Reference

ipRGCs and the Body Clock

slide the wavelength of evening light and watch blue near 480 nm drive the strongest melatonin suppression

A fifth photoreceptor — the melanopsin-bearing retinal ganglion cell — reports overall light to the circadian clock, peaking near 480 nm.

Slide the wavelength of your evening light and read where it crosses the melanopsin curve. Slide the intensity too — blue light near 480 nm suppresses melatonin most and pushes the body clock later.
